Winds around 15 miles per hour, very little rain observed. Hail fell for around 8 minutes.

Mostly pea sized hail with some quarter sized hail mixed in. Also rain around 2.4 inches over the course of an hour, 2:30-3:30 PM CDT. Culverts were observed to be running high, some fields beginning to wash out, and water almost went over the roads.
Wind combined with small hail to shread leaves on trees. Was a pause in hail, now second storm is currently producing small hail.
Relayed through social media. Noted lots of hail, mainly marble sized, and that it came down very heavy.

Reported by spotter visiting downtown Jamestown. Also reported strong winds and heavy rain.

Multiple photos were provided to the NWS showing flooding in the Jamestown area. Streets were flooded and cars in parking lots hat water up to the bottom of their doors. In addition, fields around Jamestown were also flooded.
1.50 inches of rain fell in about 30 minutes as well.
Strong winds observed, with numerous tree limbs down, some up to 4 inches in diameter. Heavy rain and hail of an unknown size also reported.

Relayed through Emergency Manager...Destructive thunderstorm winds in Marion caused two grain bins to lay over, some roofs to detach, and large evergreens to be uprooted. The town is without power, and some basements have taken on water.

Broadcast media relayed a photo of a brief landspout tornado taken from 5 miles south of Chaseley and 3 miles east of Silver Lake.

Tornado was on and off the ground for about 5 to 10 minutes as it traveled southeast. A farmstead was hit near the point of touchdown in which a camper was rolled over and a small grain bin lost its roof.

Report via social media with photos of thunderstorm wind damage including a flipped dumpster, stripped awning off a motorhome, a metal shed displaced by at least 500 feet, and a flattened chicken pen. Time estimated from radar.
Funnel touched the ground and kicked up dust. Multiple photos of the tornado were shared with the National Weather Service.

Late report from the storms of September 12. Thunderstorm winds lifted and folded back a significant portion of a pole barn roof. The winds also destroyed a calf shelter, threw wind break panels around, and a 1000 gallon water tank blew away. This was a public report passed on to the NWS by a broadcast media partner.
